ddls:
CREATE TABLE "characters" (
  "char_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each character */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the character */,
  "house_id" INTEGER /* ID of the house the character belongs to */,
  "family_id" INTEGER /* ID of the family the character belongs to */,
  "age" INTEGER /* Age of the character */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the character */,
  "alive" INTEGER /* Whether the character is alive or not */,
  "title" TEXT /* Title of the character (e.g., King, Queen, Lord) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("char_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_characters_family_id FOREIGN KEY ("family_id") REFERENCES families ("family_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_characters_house_id FOREIGN KEY ("house_id") REFERENCES houses ("house_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "houses" (
  "house_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each house */,
  "house_name" TEXT /* Name of the house */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the house */,
  "seat" TEXT /* Seat of the house */,
  "region" TEXT /* Region where the house is located */,
  "sigil" TEXT /* Sigil of the house */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("house_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "families" (
  "family_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each family */,
  "family_name" TEXT /* Name of the family */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the family */,
  "relation_to_house" TEXT /* Relation to the house (e.g., founding family, vassal family) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("family_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "interaction_types" (
  "interaction_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each interaction type */,
  "interaction_type_name" TEXT /* Name of the interaction type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the interaction type */,
  "is_positive" INTEGER /* Whether the interaction type is positive or not */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("interaction_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "interactions" (
  "interaction_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each interaction */,
  "source_char_id" INTEGER /* ID of the source character */,
  "target_char_id" INTEGER /* ID of the target character */,
  "interaction_type_id" INTEGER /* ID of the interaction type */,
  "weight" INTEGER /* Weight or strength of the interaction */,
  "direction" TEXT /* Direction of the interaction (e.g., one-way, two-way) */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the interaction */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("interaction_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_interactions_interaction_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("interaction_type_id") REFERENCES interaction_types ("interaction_type_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_interactions_target_char_id FOREIGN KEY ("target_char_id") REFERENCES characters ("char_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_interactions_source_char_id FOREIGN KEY ("source_char_id") REFERENCES characters ("char_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "character_relationships" (
  "relationship_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each relationship */,
  "char_id" INTEGER /* ID of the character */,
  "related_char_id" INTEGER /* ID of the related character */,
  "relationship_type" TEXT /* Type of relationship (e.g., family, friend, ally) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("relationship_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_character_relationships_related_char_id FOREIGN KEY ("related_char_id") REFERENCES characters ("char_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_character_relationships_char_id FOREIGN KEY ("char_id") REFERENCES characters ("char_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "network_analysis" (
  "analysis_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each analysis */,
  "char_id" INTEGER /* ID of the character */,
  "degree_centrality" REAL /* Degree centrality of the character */,
  "betweenness_centrality" REAL /* Betweenness centrality of the character */,
  "closeness_centrality" REAL /* Closeness centrality of the character */,
  "analysis_date" TEXT /* Date of the analysis */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("analysis_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_analysis_char_id FOREIGN KEY ("char_id") REFERENCES characters ("char_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "books" (
  "book_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each book */,
  "book_name" TEXT /* Name of the book */,
  "author" TEXT /* Author of the book */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Release date of the book */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("book_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "character_books" (
  "character_book_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each character-book pair */,
  "char_id" INTEGER /* ID of the character */,
  "book_id" INTEGER /* ID of the book */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("character_book_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_character_books_char_id FOREIGN KEY ("char_id") REFERENCES characters ("char_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_character_books_book_id FOREIGN KEY ("book_id") REFERENCES books ("book_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "interaction_books" (
  "interaction_book_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each interaction-book pair */,
  "interaction_id" INTEGER /* ID of the interaction */,
  "book_id" INTEGER /* ID of the book */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("interaction_book_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_interaction_books_interaction_id FOREIGN KEY ("interaction_id") REFERENCES interactions ("interaction_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_interaction_books_book_id FOREIGN KEY ("book_id") REFERENCES books ("book_id")
)
column_names: ID of the house the character belongs to
